Patch Set 5:

Arshad, *my* request was to keep the compatibility for the old function name for at least several tags, if not a longer time, otherwise this breaks every other patch for very little benefit IMHO. I'm not sure why Oleg would prefer that over applying this incrementally? We typically don't just break things with API changes, because the cost of testing and review is higher that just sending new patches to a mailing list. 

Also, given the size of this patch it will always have a large number of conflicts. 

The main objection AFAICS is to apply a time-limited compat macro before all of the code has been converted, so I propose the following:
- first patch renames function and adds unlimited compat macro and spelling.txt to start warnings
- several independent patches to do conversions of existing code
- final patch to be applied later to remove compat macro and fix any remaining users

Patch Set 5: Verified+1

Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne-arm on RHEL 8.8/aarch64, RHEL 8.9/x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest from onyx-91vm3: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/994df6c5-69bf-4ba6-988a-be0544a75a61 ran 5 tests.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> Arshad, *my* request was to keep the compatibility for the old function name for at least several tags, if not a longer time, otherwise this breaks every other patch for very little benefit IMHO. I'm not sure why Oleg would prefer that over applying this incrementally? We typically don't just break things with API changes, because the cost of testing and review is higher that just sending new patches to a mailing list. 
> 
> Also, given the size of this patch it will always have a large number of conflicts. 
> 
> The main objection AFAICS is to apply a time-limited compat macro before all of the code has been converted, so I propose the following:
> - first patch renames function and adds unlimited compat macro and spelling.txt to start warnings
> - several independent patches to do conversions of existing code
> - final patch to be applied later to remove compat macro and fix any remaining users

Andreas, apologies for the confusion. I agree with you. I was also saying the same thing. This is not new, we did CLASSERT/BUILD_BUG_ON() etc like this. Now we are sure we are doing this way. What I will do is revert it back to previous version. And then take it form there. (That it, modify time relted macro to unlimited.)

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

commit.message:
LU-16741 ptlrpc: ptlrpc: rename ptlrpc_req_finished

First series of patchs thats renames ptlrpc_req_finished
to ptlrpc_req_put

Change it as part of a general refactor of the ptlrpc
request put/freeing code.
